skill.name: conductor-validator
skill.description: Validates Conductor project directories, required documentation files, track status markers, task markers, and track ID patterns.
skill.summary: Checks Conductor project artifacts for expected structure and workflow metadata.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged shell backticks, weak cryptography, and network reconnaissance, but the reviewed file shows Markdown fences, checklist markers, and .md filenames rather than those threats. The only confirmed concern is limited Bash and filesystem use for read-only directory checks, so the skill is low risk with normal command-review caution.

low_findings.title: Read-Only Bash Commands for Project Inspection
low_findings.description: The skill permits Bash and lists fixed ls commands that inspect the conductor directory and required project files. The commands are read-only and contain no user-controlled interpolation, but they still execute external shell commands and read local project paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly includes fixed ls commands and allows Bash. Risk is limited because the commands are read-only and do not include dynamic input.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Reported as Ruby Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static Ruby backtick findings at the reported locations correspond to Markdown code fences or inline documentation references, not executable Ruby code. No Ruby file, Ruby interpreter invocation, or command substitution is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are Markdown delimiters, inline file references, or examples. They are not Ruby source code and do not execute commands.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts map to prose and Markdown checklist examples, including .md file references and task marker text. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, encryption call, or key handling is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant lines contain description text and Markdown task-marker examples. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or any cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance alerts map to Markdown status-marker examples. The skill contains no network commands, host scanning instructions, external domains, or outbound request behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are checklist examples showing in-progress markers. No network reconnaissance command or network target appears in the reviewed file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Tool Access
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill metadata allows Bash, and the body includes fixed read-only ls commands. This is a dangerous-capability pattern but not malicious in the observed context.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Bash access is explicit and the command examples are visible. The concern is capability-based because the documented commands are read-only.

content.value_statement: Conductor projects can drift when required files, track metadata, or task markers are missing. This skill checks the expected project structure and reports practical fixes.
actual_capabilities: Checks whether the conductor directory exists.
Verifies the expected top-level Conductor documentation files.
Reviews status marker formats used in tracks.md.
Reviews task marker formats used in plan.md.
Documents the expected track ID pattern.
limitations: It does not create or repair missing files by itself.
It relies on local project files being available to the assistant.
It does not validate business requirements inside each document.
It uses simple file and marker checks, not a full Conductor parser.

use_cases.title: New Project Setup Check
use_cases.description: Confirm that a new Conductor workspace includes the required directory and documentation files.

use_cases.title: Track Readiness Review
use_cases.description: Inspect track directories and marker conventions before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Troubleshooting Workflow Drift
use_cases.description: Find missing artifacts or inconsistent markers when Conductor tasks do not match expected workflow state.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Basic Validation
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner check for a local workspace
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate this repository's Conductor project structure and summarize any missing required files.

prompt_templates.title: Check Track Status Markers
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the Conductor track and task markers. Tell me which markers are valid and which entries need correction.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose Setup Problems
prompt_templates.scenario: Find causes of a broken Conductor workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect the Conductor artifacts and explain why the project setup may be incomplete or inconsistent.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare an Implementation Readiness Report
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before engineering work starts
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate the Conductor directory, required files, tracks, task markers, and track ID patterns. Provide prioritized remediation steps.

output_examples.input: Validate the Conductor setup before we start implementation.
output_examples.output: The conductor directory is present. Required files are mostly present, but workflow.md is missing. Create workflow.md before starting implementation.

output_examples.input: Check whether our track status markers are consistent.
output_examples.output: The track list uses valid complete and pending markers. One in-progress marker uses an unexpected format and should be changed to the documented in-progress marker.

output_examples.input: Tell me whether this project is ready for Conductor work.
output_examples.output: The project is not ready yet. Add the missing tracks directory, confirm the required documentation files, and use the documented track ID pattern.

best_practices: Run validation after creating or changing Conductor project files.
Review reported shell commands before allowing them to run.
Fix missing required files before using track plans for implementation.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for unrelated project management formats.
Do not treat marker validation as a review of task quality.
Do not allow extra shell commands beyond the documented read-only checks without review.

faq.question: What does this skill validate?
faq.answer: It validates the Conductor directory, required documentation files, track directories, status markers, task markers, and track ID pattern.

faq.question: Does it modify my repository?
faq.answer: No. The documented checks are read-only and focus on listing directories and required files.

faq.question: Why does it need Bash?
faq.answer: It uses simple ls commands to inspect local directories and files.

faq.question: Can it create missing Conductor files?
faq.answer: The skill does not define file creation steps. Ask your assistant separately if you want generated files.

faq.question: Can it validate task content quality?
faq.answer: It mainly validates structure and marker conventions. It does not deeply assess task scope or requirements.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
